sections.paragraph:
ἁγνευτήριον, ου, τό (s. ἁγνεύω; Chaeremon Hist. [I a.d.]: 618 Fgm. 6, p. 150, 9 Jac.=Porphyr., De Abst. 4, 6) lit. ‘place of purification’, perh. sanctuary, of the part of the temple precinct in which the ἅγια σκεύη were kept Ox 840, 8; 13 (s. Unknown Sayings 37 n. 2; 41–43: ‘inner court, court of the Israelites’).—DELG s.v. ἅζομαι. M-M s.v. ἁγνεία.

Chaeremon Chaeremon , I a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

perh. perh. = perhaps

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

Unknown Unknown Sayings = JJeremias, Unknown Sayings of Jesus—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 12
